vulnerabilities.title: Horizontal Privilege Escalation - User Profile Access
vulnerabilities.description: Authenticated users can view any user's profile by specifying arbitrary user IDs. The endpoint uses Finale auto-generated REST API with authentication (security.isAuthorized()) but no ownership validation. An attacker with a valid JWT token can enumerate and access all user profiles including email addresses, usernames, roles, profile images, and last login IPs.
vulnerabilities.root_cause: Finale REST framework generates CRUD endpoints without built-in authorization checks. The middleware only validates JWT token presence/validity but does not verify the authenticated user has permission to access the requested user ID.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario:
1. Attacker registers account (User ID 10)
2. Attacker logs in and receives JWT token
3. Attacker sends GET /api/Users/1 with their token
4. Server validates token (authentication passes)
5. Server returns User 1's profile without ownership check
6. Attacker repeats for all user IDs to enumerate database
vulnerabilities.impact: User enumeration, PII disclosure (email, username, role, IP address), GDPR/privacy violations, reconnaissance for targeted attacks
vulnerabilities.exploitability: High - Simple enumeration with valid credentials
vulnerabilities.prerequisites: Valid user account and JWT token
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Add Finale resource.read.auth hook to validate userId from token matches requested user ID
2. OR implement custom route handler with ownership check: UserModel.findOne({ where: { id: req.params.id, id: req.body.UserId } })
3. Consider if endpoint is necessary - frontend might only need /rest/user/whoami for current user

vulnerabilities.title: Horizontal Privilege Escalation - Feedback Access
vulnerabilities.description: Authenticated users can read any user's feedback by specifying arbitrary feedback IDs. The Feedback model includes a UserId field associating feedback with its author, but this relationship is never validated. An attacker can enumerate feedback IDs and access comments and ratings from all users.
vulnerabilities.root_cause: Finale auto-generates GET /api/Feedbacks/:id endpoint without ownership validation. The security.isAuthorized() middleware only verifies JWT validity, not whether the authenticated user owns the requested feedback. No resource.read.auth hook is configured to enforce access control.
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario:
1. Victim submits feedback via POST /api/Feedbacks (Feedback ID 42, UserId 5)
2. Attacker logs in with their account (UserId 10)
3. Attacker sends GET /api/Feedbacks/42 with their JWT token
4. Server validates attacker's token (authentication succeeds)
5. Server returns victim's feedback including comment, rating, and UserId
6. Attacker can enumerate all feedback IDs to extract all feedback data
vulnerabilities.impact: Privacy violation - users' feedback comments and ratings exposed to other users. Feedback may contain sensitive information, opinions, or complaints. Breaks user expectation of privacy for submitted feedback.
vulnerabilities.exploitability: High - Requires only valid credentials and sequential ID enumeration
vulnerabilities.prerequisites: Valid user account and JWT token
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Add Finale resource.read.auth hook to validate feedback.UserId matches authenticated user's ID
2. OR implement query filter: FeedbackModel.findOne({ where: { id: req.params.id, UserId: req.body.UserId } })
3. Consider business requirement: should users access their own feedback or is it write-only/admin-only?

vulnerabilities.title: Unauthenticated Basket Coupon Manipulation - Critical
vulnerabilities.description: The coupon application endpoint has NO authentication or authorization. Any user (including unauthenticated attackers) can apply or remove coupons to/from ANY basket by specifying the basket ID in the URL. This allows discount fraud, financial loss, and denial of service attacks.
vulnerabilities.root_cause:
Three-layer security failure:
1. Route definition (server.ts:598) has NO security.isAuthorized() middleware
2. Route definition has NO security.appendUserId() middleware to inject owner ID
3. Handler (routes/coupon.ts:18) uses BasketModel.findByPk(id) which fetches ANY basket by ID without ownership validation
4. Handler updates basket.coupon directly without checking basket.UserId against authenticated user
vulnerabilities.attack_scenario:
Scenario 1 - Unauthenticated Fraud:
1. Attacker discovers target has Basket ID 5
2. Attacker sends PUT /rest/basket/5/coupon/JUICE90 (no auth token)
3. Application applies 90% discount to victim's basket
4. Attacker repeats for their own baskets for free products

Scenario 2 - Denial of Service:
1. Attacker enumerates basket IDs (1-1000)
2. Attacker applies invalid coupon 'XXXXX' to all baskets
3. Legitimate users receive 404 'Invalid coupon' on checkout

Scenario 3 - Cross-User Manipulation:
1. User A applies premium coupon to their basket
2. User B removes coupon from User A's basket
3. User A loses discount without explanation
vulnerabilities.impact:
CRITICAL financial and business impact:
- Revenue Loss: Unauthorized discount application
- Fraud: Self-service coupon codes without restrictions
- No Audit Trail: Unauthenticated = no user accountability
- Business Logic Bypass: Coupon validation circumvented
- Denial of Service: Attackers can sabotage competitor baskets
- Reputation Damage: Users affected by coupon manipulation
vulnerabilities.exploitability: Critical - No credentials required, trivial to exploit with simple HTTP requests
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
